title: cerastium holosteoides var. vulgare
intro: Cerastium holosteoides var. vulgare, also known as the mouse-ear chickweed, is a type of flowering plant in the carnation family that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cerastium holosteoides var. vulgare include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
